{"query":"Japan Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi Yasukuni Shrine visit 2026 date announcement","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1426,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://japan-forward.com/yasukuni-shrine-returns-to-spotlight-as-takaichi-government-takes-off/","title":"Yasukuni Shrine Returns to Spotlight as Takaichi Government Takes Off | JAPAN Forward","content":"On October 17, Takaichi, leader of the ruling Liberal Democratic Party (LDP), sent a ritual offering during the shrine's three-day Annual Fall Rites but chose not to visit in person.
At his inaugural press conference on Wednesday, Shinjiro Koizumi, the new Defense Minister, said, "It's true I have paid respect there every year on the anniversary of the end of the war."
Minoru Kihara, now Chief Cabinet Secretary, made an offering at the shrine while serving as defense minister, but has not indicated whether he will do so under the Takaichi administration.
Meanwhile, over 60 lawmakers from the LDP, Nippon Ishin no Kai, and Democratic Party for the People paid their respects during this year's Fall Rites.
Fumitake Fujita, co-head of the Nippon Ishin no Kai, which recently formed a coalition with the LDP, said on a television program on October 5, "This [Yasukuni visit] should not be made into a diplomatic issue."
But the shrine became a source of political and diplomatic controversy after 14 so-called "Class A war criminals" from the Second World War were enshrined in 1978.
When the information became public in the subsequent year, China and South Korea began lodging strong protests, accusing Japan of "honoring war criminals.""},{"url":"https://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2026/04/17/japan/politics/takaichi-yasukuni-visit-skip/","title":"Takaichi to skip Yasukuni visit during spring festival - The Japan Times","content":"Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is considering skipping a visit to the war-linked Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo during its three-day spring festival that starts Tuesday, informed sources said Friday.
Takaichi, part of the conservative wing in the ruling Liberal Democratic Party, indicated her stance on visiting Yasukuni Shrine on a television program after the party’s landslide victory in the House of Representatives election in February.
Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara said at a news conference Friday that he will make an appropriate decision on whether to visit the shrine.
Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi, who has regularly visited the shrine on the Aug."},{"url":"https://japan.kantei.go.jp/105/speech/202606/0623okinawa.html","title":"Address by Prime Minister TAKAICHI Sanae for the Memorial Ceremony to Commemorate the Fallen on the 81st Anniversary of the End of the Battle of Okinawa | Speeches | Prime Minister's Office of Jap","content":"Address by Prime Minister TAKAICHI Sanae for the Memorial Ceremony to Commemorate the Fallen on the 81st Anniversary of the End of the Battle of Okinawa"},{"url":"https://www.mofa.go.jp/na/na1/us/pageite_000001_01530.html","title":"Prime Minister TAKAICHI’s Visit to the U.S. (March 18-21, 2026) | Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan","content":"Mar 19, 2026 — Japan-United States of America Relations. Summit Meeting and Dinner Reception (March 19, 2026)"},{"url":"https://www.thehindu.com/news/international/japan-pm-takaichi-sends-offering-to-yasukuni-war-shrine/article70888045.ece","title":"Japan PM Takaichi sends offering to Yasukuni war shrine - The Hindu","content":"Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi sent a ritual offering on Tuesday (April 21, 2026) to a shrine honouring the country's war dead that has long angered neighbouring countries, but did not visit in person, media reports and an unidentified source close to the matter said.
"Our government urges Japan's responsible leaders to face history squarely and to demonstrate, through action, a humble reflection on and a sincere remorse for its past," Foreign Ministry spokesman Park Il told reporters.
Japan's top government spokesman Minoru Kihara declined to comment on the reports.

Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is considering skipping a visit to the war-linked Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo during its three-day","score":0.9020676,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/japanespecials/posts/japans-prime-minister-sanae-takaichi-sent-a-traditional-masakaki-offering-to-yas/1622273862624166","title":"Japan ESPEcials - Japan’s Prime Minister, Sanae Takaichi,...","content":"## Japan ESPEcials's Post. ### **Japan ESPEcials**. Japan’s Prime Minister, Sanae Takaichi, sent a traditional “masakaki” offering to Yasukuni Shrine on April 21, 2026, marking the shrine’s annual spring festival. She is not expected to visit the shrine in person during the event, following a cautious approach similar to recent leaders. The Yasukuni Shrine, which honors Japan’s war dead, often draws international attention during such observances. While many in Japan view the rituals as cultural and commemorative traditions, some neighboring countries, including China and South Korea, have expressed concern due to historical interpretations linked to the site. Respected Her Excellency Hon The Great Powerful Peace 🕊️ Lover Prime Minister's Office of Japan Prime Minister's Office of Japan successful gd morning.Three United Nations India at UN (Permanent Mission of India to the United Nations, New York) United Nations Peacekeeping Nation visit give to all sides development and World 🌍 Peace 🕊️.","score":0.72019166,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.straitstimes.com/asia/east-asia/japan-pm-takaichi-sends-offering-to-war-linked-yasukuni-shrine-for-spring-rite","title":"Japan PM Takaichi sends offering to war-linked Yasukuni Shrine for spring rite | The Straits Times","content":"The Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo is viewed by China and South Korea as a symbol of Japan’s wartime militarism. TOKYO – Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi sent a ritual offering to Yasukuni Shrine in Tokyo on April 21as the shrine, viewed by China and South Korea as a symbol of Japan’s wartime militarism, started its three-day spring festival. Ms Takaichi, who regularly visited the shrine before taking office in October 2025, is unlikely to do so during the festival this time, a source close to her said, as worsened ties with China following her November remarks on how Japan might respond to a Taiwan emergency show little sign of improvement. Visits to the shrine by Japanese leaders and ministers have long been a source of diplomatic friction with neighbouring countries because it honours wartime leaders convicted as war criminals by a post-World War II international tribunal, along with millions of war dead. A wooden plaque, sent as a ritual offering on April 21 for the war dead at Yasukuni Shrine, bearing the name of Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi.","score":0.6188962,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.06,"request_id":"0d1754de-81ca-442e-93a3-6199dab9498e"}
